edges.description: TP53 alteration permits genomically unstable progression to high-grade disease
edges.target: TP53 Loss and p53-Mediated DNA Damage Response Failure

edges.description: TERT promoter mutation supports immortalization during tumor progression
PI3K pathway activation adds a parallel survival and growth program
MAPK-driven tumor evolution culminates in loss of differentiated thyroid functions
edges.target: Dedifferentiation and Loss of Thyroid Identity

edges.description: BRAF V600E-mutant tumors show higher tumor-cell PD-L1 positivity, coupling MAPK driver status to adaptive immune resistance
edges.target: Adaptive Immune Resistance and Immunosuppressive Microenvironment

edges.description: TP53 loss facilitates progression into less-differentiated undifferentiated disease
edges.source: TP53 Loss and p53-Mediated DNA Damage Response Failure
edges.target: Dedifferentiation and Loss of Thyroid Identity

edges.description: TERT activation sustains clonal expansion of less-differentiated tumor cells
edges.target: Dedifferentiation and Loss of Thyroid Identity

edges.description: PI3K-AKT signaling supports invasive and metastatic behavior
Dedifferentiated tumors acquire a more invasive and metastatic phenotype
edges.source: Dedifferentiation and Loss of Thyroid Identity

edges.description: Aggressive tumor growth and local invasion present as a rapidly enlarging anterior thyroid or neck mass.
Local invasion and compressive neck disease can impair swallowing.
Pulmonary metastases or compressive neck disease can produce shortness of breath.
Critical upper-airway narrowing from local tumor invasion or compression can produce stridor.
Invasion or compression of laryngeal structures can cause hoarseness.
intermediate_mechanisms: laryngeal or recurrent laryngeal nerve involvement

edges.description: Invasive ATC frequently spreads to regional cervical lymph nodes.

edges.target: Adaptive Immune Resistance and Immunosuppressive Microenvironment

nodes.description: ATC frequently upregulates tumor-cell PD-L1 within an immune-infiltrated microenvironment. The majority of ATCs are PD-L1-positive and contain abundant CD3+/CD8+ tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes and tumor-associated macrophages, indicating an "immune-adapted" tumor in which pre-existing anti-tumor immunity is actively suppressed through the PD-1/PD-L1 axis. PD-L1 positivity is enriched in BRAF V600E-mutant ATC and is considered predictive of response to anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy.
nodes.id: Adaptive Immune Resistance and Immunosuppressive Microenvironment

biological_processes: Negative Regulation of T Cell Mediated Immunity

evidence.reference_title: PD-L1 Expression and Its Modulating Factors in Anaplastic Thyroid Carcinoma: A Multi-institutional Study.
evidence.snippet: Most ATCs (73.2%) were PD-L1-positive.

evidence.reference_title: PD-L1 Expression and Its Modulating Factors in Anaplastic Thyroid Carcinoma: A Multi-institutional Study.
evidence.snippet: Tumor cell surface PD-L1 expression is considered predictive of therapeutic response.

evidence.reference_title: PD-L1 expression and immune cells in anaplastic carcinoma and poorly differentiated carcinoma of the human thyroid gland: A retrospective study.
evidence.snippet: In addition to increased PD-L1 expression, all ATC cases exhibited significantly increased CD3+ and CD8+ T cells, CD68+ and CD163+ macrophages, and S100+ dendritic cells compared with the PDTC cases.

nodes.description: Regional nodal involvement is common in locoregionally advanced disease.

evidence.reference_title: Anaplastic Thyroid Cancer.
evidence.snippet: anaplastic thyroid cancer is highly locally invasive, with a propensity for early lymph node positivity and distant metastatic disease.

nodes.description: Combined BRAF and MEK inhibition provides substantial clinical benefit in BRAF V600E-mutant ATC and is a core systemic option for molecularly selected patients.

evidence.reference_title: Dabrafenib plus trametinib in patients with BRAF V600E-mutant anaplastic thyroid cancer: updated analysis from the phase II ROAR basket study.
evidence.snippet: The investigator-assessed ORR was 56% (95% confidence interval, 38.1% to 72.1%), including three complete responses; the 12-month DOR rate was 50%.

evidence.reference_title: Dabrafenib plus trametinib in patients with BRAF V600E-mutant anaplastic thyroid cancer: updated analysis from the phase II ROAR basket study.
evidence.snippet: These updated results confirm the substantial clinical benefit and manageable toxicity of dabrafenib plus trametinib in BRAF V600E-mutant ATC.

nodes.description: As follicular-cell-derived thyroid carcinoma dedifferentiates into ATC, it loses thyroid-specific transcriptional programs and iodide-handling functions. This transition is a major reason ATC is radioiodine refractory and clinically far more aggressive than differentiated thyroid cancer.
nodes.id: Dedifferentiation and Loss of Thyroid Identity

evidence.reference_title: Dedifferentiation and Redifferentiation of Follicular-Cell-Derived Thyroid Carcinoma: Mechanisms and Therapeutic Implications.
evidence.snippet: Follicular-cell-derived thyroid carcinoma, while typically associated with a favorable prognosis, can undergo dedifferentiation into poorly differentiated (PDTC) or anaplastic thyroid carcinoma (ATC), leading to enhanced aggressiveness and radioiodine resistance.

evidence.reference_title: Dedifferentiation and Redifferentiation of Follicular-Cell-Derived Thyroid Carcinoma: Mechanisms and Therapeutic Implications.
evidence.snippet: These alterations promote the loss of thyroid-specific functions, including iodide metabolism, and correlate with poor clinical outcomes.

nodes.description: Esophageal compression or invasion commonly produces difficulty swallowing.

evidence.reference_title: Update on current diagnosis and management of anaplastic thyroid carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: It is the most aggressive form of thyroid carcinoma, with a median survival of 5 mo and poor quality of life (airway obstruction, dysphagia, hoarseness, persistent pain).

nodes.description: Tracheal compression, airway narrowing, or pulmonary metastatic disease may cause shortness of breath.

evidence.reference_title: Anaplastic thyroid carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Systemic symptoms include anorexia, weight loss, and shortness of breath with pulmonary metastases.

nodes.description: Recurrent laryngeal nerve involvement or laryngeal invasion often causes dysphonia.

evidence.reference_title: Update on current diagnosis and management of anaplastic thyroid carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: It is the most aggressive form of thyroid carcinoma, with a median survival of 5 mo and poor quality of life (airway obstruction, dysphagia, hoarseness, persistent pain).

nodes.description: Combination of the multikinase inhibitor lenvatinib with the anti-PD-1 immune checkpoint inhibitor pembrolizumab is an emerging systemic option for anaplastic and poorly differentiated thyroid carcinoma. It exploits the high tumor mutational burden and elevated PD-L1 expression characteristic of these dedifferentiated tumors, with the most durable responses seen in tumors with increased TMB or a high PD-L1 tumor proportion score.

evidence.reference_title: Combination of Lenvatinib and Pembrolizumab Is an Effective Treatment Option for Anaplastic and Poorly Differentiated Thyroid Carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: These tumor properties implicate responsiveness to antiangiogenic and antiproliferative multikinase inhibitors such as lenvatinib, and immune checkpoint inhibitors such as pembrolizumab.

evidence.reference_title: Combination of Lenvatinib and Pembrolizumab Is an Effective Treatment Option for Anaplastic and Poorly Differentiated Thyroid Carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Our results implicate that the combination of lenvatinib and pembrolizumab might be safe and effective in patients with ATC/PDTC and can result in complete and long-term remissions.

nodes.description: ATC commonly arises from a pre-existing follicular-cell-derived thyroid carcinoma that already carries a truncal MAPK-pathway driver alteration. This initiating event establishes the core oncogenic signaling axis that is later exploited therapeutically in molecularly selected tumors.

evidence.reference_title: NOVEL INSIGHTS IN ADVANCED THYROID CARCINOMA: FROM MECHANISMS TO TREATMENTS: Molecular insights into the origin, biology, and treatment of anaplastic thyroid carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: The sequence of events leading to the development of ATC commonly begins with a tumorigenic mutation that constitutively activates the mitogen-activated protein kinase (MAPK) pathway, giving rise to indolent entities such as well-differentiated papillary or follicular thyroid carcinomas.

nodes.description: ATC exhibits marked dysregulation of matrix metalloproteinase-related programs, remodeling the extracellular environment to support migration, local tissue destruction, and distant spread.

evidence.reference_title: Genome-wide expression analysis suggests a crucial role of dysregulation of matrix metalloproteinases pathway in undifferentiated thyroid carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Inhibition of matrix metalloproteinases pathway is a major event involved in thyroid cancer progression and its dysregulation may result crucial for invasiveness, migration and metastasis.

nodes.description: Aberrant PI3K-PTEN-AKT signaling provides a parallel survival and growth pathway during ATC progression, helping aggressive clones persist despite loss of differentiated thyroid-cell features.

evidence.reference_title: Somatic genetic alterations in the development and progression in thyroid tumors of follicular cells.
evidence.snippet: Progression of thyroid tumors to advanced and less-differentiated carcinomas requires additional oncogenic alterations, including TP53 and TERT promoter mutation, and aberrant PI3K-PTEN-AKT signaling.

nodes.description: Patients frequently present with a rapidly enlarging fixed thyroid or neck mass reflecting explosive local growth.

evidence.reference_title: Anaplastic Thyroid Cancer.
evidence.snippet: Anaplastic thyroid cancer typically presents as a rapidly growing anterior neck mass and may have compressive symptoms early in the disease.

nodes.description: Critical upper-airway narrowing can produce inspiratory stridor and mandate urgent airway intervention.

evidence.reference_title: Anaplastic thyroid carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Locally, ATC shows a rapidly enlarging anterior neck mass, with accompanying dysphagia (40%), voice change or hoarseness (40%), and stridor (24%).

nodes.description: TERT promoter mutation is a late progression event that reactivates telomerase, enabling telomere maintenance and sustained clonal expansion in advanced follicular-cell-derived thyroid carcinoma.

evidence.reference_title: Somatic genetic alterations in the development and progression in thyroid tumors of follicular cells.
evidence.snippet: Progression of thyroid tumors to advanced and less-differentiated carcinomas requires additional oncogenic alterations, including TP53 and TERT promoter mutation, and aberrant PI3K-PTEN-AKT signaling.

nodes.description: Progression to ATC commonly includes late TP53 alteration, which weakens p53-mediated DNA damage control and permits genomically unstable evolution toward highly aggressive undifferentiated disease.
nodes.id: TP53 Loss and p53-Mediated DNA Damage Response Failure

biological_processes: DNA damage response, signal transduction by p53 class mediator

evidence.reference_title: Somatic genetic alterations in the development and progression in thyroid tumors of follicular cells.
evidence.snippet: Progression of thyroid tumors to advanced and less-differentiated carcinomas requires additional oncogenic alterations, including TP53 and TERT promoter mutation, and aberrant PI3K-PTEN-AKT signaling.
